title: Phase 1: fix real bugs (crash/data-loss)
description:
Panics/data-loss on real inputs. See notes/idiomatic-rust-audit.md Phase 1.

- migration/src/m20220930_011056_seed_positions.rs:43-45 — down() wipes entire player table (no filter); scope or remove.
- migration/src/m20221112_151717_create_trade_tables.rs:162-167,168-173 — TeamTrade FK cols marked .auto_increment() (copy-paste); drop it.
- entity/src/entities/draft_pick_option.rs:126-146 — transition HashMap omits InvalidatedByExternalTrade variant; replace with exhaustive match. :176-180 panic on missing key -> Err.
- entity/src/entities/trade.rs:243,258 — double-unwrap on Option<i64> panics on explicit None; match/handle.
- entity/src/entities/contract/annual_contract_advancement.rs:141,145 — Ok(1) sentinel for TBD salary collides with real $1; return Option<i16>. :160 Decimal->String->i16 parse().unwrap(); use to_i16() + propagate.
- logic/src/deadline_processing/roster_lock/validate_rosters.rs:177,188,197,221 — team_contracts[0].team_id.unwrap() panic; pass team_id: i64 in.
- logic/src/auction/fa_auction.rs:20,42 — maybe_override_effective_date param never read; wire in or drop.
- logic/src/annual_contract_advancement/create_team_contracts_for_annual_advancement.rs:31 — .expect on team_id; ok_or_else.
- import-data: import_players.rs:197-260 (~20 unwraps on NBA JSON), process_seasonal_transactions.rs:110,160-161 (OOB index), csv_transaction_utils.rs:10-22 (silent empty/panic), seasonal_keeper_deadline_contracts.rs:186 (panic in filter_map), import_original_team_contracts.rs:60,115, plus parse().unwrap() at import_teams.rs:121/import_draft_picks.rs:53/parse_draft_picks.rs:57 — propagate or skip-and-log per row.
close_reason: Phase 1 real-bug fixes shipped. Migration: seed_positions down() no longer wipes player table (removed unfiltered delete_many); dropped bogus .auto_increment() on TeamTrade FK cols. Entity: draft_pick_option status transitions now an exhaustive match (no missing-variant panic); trade.rs double-unwrap on Set(None) guarded via let-Some; annual_contract_advancement salary .to_string().parse().unwrap() -> to_i16() propagate (i16-overflow crash). Logic: validate_rosters team_contracts[0].team_id.unwrap() -> threaded team_id:i64 param; fa_auction maybe_override_effective_date wired through sign_auction_contract_to_team (FA effective_date now honors override; veteran passes None, behavior preserved); create_team_contracts .expect(team_id) -> ok_or_else propagate. import-data: ~30 unwrap/panic/OOB sites across import_players (NBA JSON), process_seasonal_transactions (deadline OOB), csv_transaction_utils, seasonal_keeper_deadline_contracts (filter_map panic), import_original_team_contracts, import_teams/import_draft_picks/parse_draft_picks -> propagate-or-skip-and-log (delegated to subagent, diff reviewed). Verified: cargo build + test (37 pass) + clippy (0 warnings from changes) green on workspace; import-data cargo build green. Deferred Ok(1) TBD-salary sentinel to fbkl-rust-0kb.6 (needs nullable column = Phase 4).

title: GraphQL API surface (team/player/contract/trade/draft/auction/...)
description: Expose rules engine via GraphQL per notes/implementation-specs/06-graphql-api-surface.md.

title: Deadline scheduler + transaction-processor (infra)
description: Build orchestration layer per notes/implementation-specs/05-deadline-scheduler-and-transaction-processor.md. Rules §4.2, §8.1, §14.4.
close_reason: Implemented spec 05: job_run table + idempotent claim, transaction-processor dispatcher, jobs scheduler wired into fbkl-server, get_salary_cap uncapped window, lock_rosters/trade error-handling fixes. Frontend commissioner console deferred — blocked on GraphQL surface (fbkl-rust-56c). Sub-event discovery deferred to specs 01/03 (fbkl-rust-lcc, fbkl-rust-1dk).

title: Rookie draft engine (order, lottery, selections)
description: Implement per notes/implementation-specs/02-rookie-draft-engine.md. Rules §7, §1.4.

title: Live auction engine (veteran + in-season FA)
description: Implement live bid engine per spec notes/implementation-specs/01-live-auction-engine.md. Covers rules §6, §8.

title: Phase 2: stop DbErr leaking to GraphQL clients + error-swallowing
description:
Systemic. See notes/idiomatic-rust-audit.md Phase 2.

Every GraphQL resolver ?s raw DbErr -> async_graphql::Error via blanket From<Display>, sending unredacted internal DB error text to clients, bypassing FbklError redaction in server/src/error.rs. Return Result<T, FbklError> from resolvers, convert at boundary. Sites: server/src/graphql/league/league_types.rs:37-47; contract/contract_types.rs:83,88; team/team_types.rs:55-60,70-77; player/player_types.rs:53-62,120-125,131-136; league/league_resolvers.rs:65,87,96-108.

Error-swallowing (DB outage looks like not-logged-in): server/src/session.rs:23 get_current_user unwrap_or_default -> return Result<Option<Model>, FbklError>; downstream login_handlers.rs:86, league_types.rs:56-58.

title: Idiomatic Rust audit remediation
description: Full-workspace audit against the idiomatic-rust skill (judgment-level idioms; clippy pedantic+nursery already clean). Findings and full file:line fix list in notes/idiomatic-rust-audit.md. Five phases: (1) real bugs, (2) security/systemic DbErr leaks, (3) error architecture, (4) illegal-states-unrepresentable type modeling, (5) polish.

title: Fresh replay dies at EoSY2015 PreseasonStart: duplicate unique-active-contract violation
description: Split out from fbkl-rust-7eb. A fresh import replay (migration refresh + real-world + all-league-data) dies at EoSY2015 PreseasonStart during advance_league_contracts with a duplicate-key violation on the contract_unique_active_contract_per_player_per_league index (migration m20221023_002183_create_contract.rs:126; insert at contract_queries.rs:412). Collision created during the advance_contract chain replacement (advance_league_contracts.rs:37 -> advance_contract -> add_replacement_contract_to_chain): two Active contracts end up sharing (league_id, end_of_season_year, player_id). Means a clean replay can't even reach the originally-reported 2018 deadline-109 error, so 7eb's 196/195 report came from a non-fresh DB. ROOT CAUSE NOT YET PINNED - needs a live import run against Postgres to diagnose (static reasoning insufficient). Likely candidates: import seeds an eosy N+1 contract that then collides with advancement from N, or advancement runs twice / over a player with two active contracts in one eosy.

title: GitHub Actions CD: path-filtered migrate (gated) -> deploy Lambdas + Pages on main
description:
## Context
Continuous deployment on push to `main`: path-filter changes, run gated migrations against Neon's direct endpoint, build+deploy the three Lambdas (arm64), and deploy both Pages apps — backend and frontend flows independent. AWS auth via OIDC (no stored keys). Rust build caching is mandatory to stay within free Actions minutes.

## Files / locations
- New `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`.
- Consumes secrets/role from the Neon (T6), Lambda (T3/T4/T5), Pages (T8), and Sentry (T9) tasks.

## What to do
1. Trigger on `push: branches: [main]`; `concurrency: deploy-${{ github.ref }}` cancel-in-progress.
2. `changes` job (dorny/paths-filter): `backend` (server/logic/entity/auth/jobs/transaction-processor/migration/lambdas/Cargo.*) and `webapp` (webapp-*).
3. `migrate` job (if backend changed): `environment: production` for a manual approval gate; run `cd migration && cargo run` with `DATABASE_URL=${{ secrets.NEON_DIRECT_URL }}` (DIRECT endpoint).
4. `deploy-lambdas` job (needs migrate): OIDC (`permissions: id-token: write`), `aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials` assuming `AWS_DEPLOY_ROLE`; install cargo-lambda; `cargo lambda build --release --arm64`; deploy `fbkl-api --enable-function-url`, `fbkl-scheduler`, `fbkl-session-gc`. Use `Swatinem/rust-cache`.
5. `deploy-pages` job (if webapp changed, parallel): pnpm install + build each app; `cloudflare/wrangler-action` `pages deploy` for both projects.
6. Add a Sentry release-tag + source-map upload step (uses `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN`).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Pushing a backend-only change runs migrate (after approval) → deploys all three Lambdas; skips Pages.
- [ ] Pushing a frontend-only change deploys Pages; skips backend.
- [ ] Migrations run against the DIRECT endpoint; deploy uses OIDC (no long-lived AWS keys in repo).
- [ ] Rust cache hits keep build time reasonable on the private-repo free minutes.

## Verification
Open a PR with a trivial backend change, merge to main, watch the workflow: approval gate appears, migrate runs against direct endpoint, Lambdas deploy. Repeat with a frontend-only change and confirm only Pages deploys.

## Notes / gotchas
- Ordering: migrate MUST precede deploy-lambdas, and migrations MUST be backward-compatible (expand-contract) since the old Lambda may still serve during rollout. Never drop a column in the same deploy that stops using it.
- Secrets to set: `AWS_DEPLOY_ROLE`, `NEON_DIRECT_URL`, `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN`, `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID`, `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN`. `SESSION_SECRET` + pooled `FBKL_DATABASE_URL` live in Lambda config, not CI.
- arm64 cross-compile needs zig (cargo-lambda manages it) — use the cargo-lambda setup action.
notes: Written: .github/workflows/deploy.yml — push-to-main, path-filtered (dorny/paths-filter). Jobs: migrate (env production gate, runs fbkl-migration up + migrate_sessions vs NEON_DATABASE_URL_DIRECT) -> deploy-lambdas (OIDC assume AWS_DEPLOY_ROLE_ARN, cargo lambda build arm64, update-function-code x3) ; deploy-app/deploy-public (pnpm build + wrangler pages deploy). Requires GH vars/secrets + production environment (see infra/README CI section).
close_reason: Serverless deploy epic complete: Lambdas live, EventBridge schedules firing, Neon DB, Pages + same-origin proxy auth, GitHub Actions CD.

title: Cloudflare Pages deploy for both Vite apps + SPA fallback + cross-origin auth
description:
## Context
Both Vite apps (`webapp-logged-in` SPA via TanStack Router, `webapp-public`) deploy to Cloudflare Pages (free, global CDN, native SPA fallback). The logged-in SPA authenticates against the API Lambda's Function URL, which is cross-origin — the real work here is the SPA fallback file plus correct cross-origin cookie/CORS wiring.

## Files / locations
- `webapp-logged-in/public/_redirects` (new) and `webapp-public/public/_redirects` if needed.
- urql/fetch client config in `webapp-logged-in` (set `credentials: "include"`).
- Vite env: API base URL (`VITE_*`) baked at build time.
- Coordinates with `fbkl-api` Function URL CORS settings.

## What to do
1. Add `_redirects` with `/*    /index.html   200` to the logged-in SPA's `public/` so deep links / refresh on client routes return index.html (200). Confirm Pages picks it up from `dist/`.
2. Create two Pages projects (logged-in, public). Configure build output dirs.
3. Set the urql client to send credentials: `fetchOptions: { credentials: "include" }` (or equivalent), and point it at the API base URL via a `VITE_` env var.
4. Configure the API Lambda Function URL CORS: `Allow-Origin` = exact Pages domain (not `*`), `Allow-Credentials: true`, allowed methods/headers for GraphQL.
5. Strongly prefer same parent domain: `app.<domain>` (Pages) + `api.<domain>` (Function URL via custom domain) to keep cookies first-party and simplify CORS.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Both apps deploy to Pages and load.
- [ ] Deep-linking/refresh on a logged-in client route (e.g. a team page) returns the app, not a 404.
- [ ] Login from the SPA sets the cookie and authenticated GraphQL queries succeed cross-origin (no silent 401s).

## Verification
Deploy, open the logged-in app, log in, navigate to a deep route, hard-refresh — session persists and data loads. Check browser devtools: cookie present, `credentials` sent, no CORS errors.

## Notes / gotchas
- The #1 failure mode is cross-origin cookies: needs ALL of `SameSite=None; Secure` (server, already set) + exact-origin CORS + `Allow-Credentials: true` + client `credentials: "include"`. Missing any one → 401s that look like a Pages bug but aren't.
- Pages handles SPA fallback natively via `_redirects`; this is exactly what R2 could not do without a Worker (reason we chose Pages).
notes: Switched to SAME-ORIGIN design: webapp-logged-in/functions/api/[[path]].ts Pages Function reverse-proxies /api/* to Lambda Function URL (first-party cookies, no CORS). SPA keeps relative /api/gql. cloudflare.tf sets Pages env var API_URL=function_url (prod+preview). Removed Function URL CORS block. Pending re-apply + wrangler deploy.
close_reason: Serverless deploy epic complete: Lambdas live, EventBridge schedules firing, Neon DB, Pages + same-origin proxy auth, GitHub Actions CD.

title: Provision Neon Postgres: pooled + direct endpoints, secrets, migration run
description:
## Context
Neon is the managed Postgres (always-free tier). Lambdas connect via the POOLED endpoint; schema migrations and the tower-sessions store migration run via the DIRECT endpoint (PgBouncer transaction mode breaks DDL + SeaORM/tower-sessions advisory locks). Same AWS region as the Lambdas (us-east-1) to minimize latency.

## Files / locations
- `migration/` crate (existing SeaORM migrations; run via `cargo run`).
- Lambda env config + GitHub Actions secrets (consumed by the CD task).

## What to do
1. Create a Neon project in the Lambda region. Capture both connection strings:
   - POOLED (`-pooler` host) → used as `FBKL_DATABASE_URL` in all Lambdas.
   - DIRECT → used as `DATABASE_URL` for the `migration/` crate and the one-time `session_store.migrate()`.
2. Run `cd migration && cargo run` against the DIRECT endpoint to create the schema.
3. Run the tower-sessions `PostgresStore::migrate()` once (a tiny bin or a guarded step) against DIRECT so the session table exists.
4. Verify Neon's configured `max_connections` for the chosen compute size; confirm reserved Lambda concurrency stays well under `default_pool_size` (≈0.9 × max_connections).
5. Store secrets: `FBKL_DATABASE_URL` (pooled) in Lambda config; `NEON_DIRECT_URL` (direct) in GitHub Actions secrets.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Neon project live; both endpoints captured.
- [ ] App schema migrated via DIRECT endpoint; `bd`-tracked migrations all applied.
- [ ] tower-sessions table exists.
- [ ] Documented: pooled host for app, direct host for migrations; Neon `max_connections` value recorded vs the concurrency cap.

## Verification
Connect via the pooled endpoint and run a trivial query through SeaORM (e.g. from `cargo lambda watch` locally). Confirm DDL ran by listing tables on the direct endpoint.

## Notes / gotchas
- NEVER point Lambdas at the direct endpoint — that path fails fast at `max_connections` with no queue.
- Migrations must be backward-compatible (expand-contract) — see CD task.
notes: DONE except session table: Neon project+DB created, app migrations ran (user confirmed). Added server bin migrate_sessions (PostgresStore::migrate via DATABASE_URL direct) to create tower_sessions table — run it to finish .6. FBKL_DATABASE_URL pooler wired into lambdas at apply time.
close_reason: Neon provisioned (pooled+direct); app migrations + migrate_sessions run against direct endpoint.

title: fbkl-scheduler Lambda: run_scheduler_tick on EventBridge (1-min)
description:
## Context
Replaces the in-process 30s poll (`jobs::spawn_scheduler`) with a Lambda invoked every minute by EventBridge Scheduler. The body calls the already-direct-callable `jobs::run_scheduler_tick(&db)`. Idempotency is guaranteed by the transaction-processor's `job_run` claims, so EventBridge double-fires are safe.

## Files / locations
- New `lambdas/src/bin/scheduler.rs`.
- Calls `fbkl_jobs::run_scheduler_tick(lambdas::db().await)`.

## What to do
1. `lambda_runtime::run(service_fn(handler))`; `handler(_: LambdaEvent<Value>)` calls `run_scheduler_tick(db().await).await?`, logs the `TickSummary`, returns `Ok(())`.
2. Configure Lambda: arm64, `provided.al2023`, `FBKL_DATABASE_URL` (pooled). Modest reserved concurrency (1–2 is enough; one tick at a time is fine).
3. (EventBridge schedule wiring lives in the EventBridge task, which depends on this.)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `cargo lambda build` produces the scheduler bin.
- [ ] Manual invoke runs one tick and logs processed/failed/skipped/errors counts.
- [ ] Re-invoking on an already-processed deadline is a no-op (AlreadyProcessed), proving idempotency holds under repeated fires.

## Verification
Invoke the deployed function with an empty event; check CloudWatch logs for the `scheduler tick` summary. Seed a due deadline in a test DB and confirm it processes once across two back-to-back invokes.

## Notes / gotchas
- Cadence drops from 30s → 1-min minimum (accepted decision). Auction/RFA sub-event synthesis (TODOs in `run_scheduler_tick`) is out of scope here — only wire what exists.
- Do NOT use `spawn_scheduler` (the perpetual loop) — Lambda can't run a forever loop; call the one-shot tick.
close_reason: Serverless deploy epic complete: Lambdas live, EventBridge schedules firing, Neon DB, Pages + same-origin proxy auth, GitHub Actions CD.

title: Create lambdas crate + Lambda-tuned Neon pooled DB helper (OnceCell)
description:
## Context
All three Lambdas share one DB-connection strategy. Lambda concurrency causes Postgres connection explosion unless we (a) connect through Neon's PgBouncer pooler, (b) hold ≤1 connection per instance, and (c) reuse the connection across warm invocations. This task creates the new `lambdas` crate and the shared DB helper used by every Lambda bin.

## Files / locations
- New crate `lambdas/` (add to workspace `members` in root `Cargo.toml`). Three `[[bin]]` targets land in later tasks; this task sets up the crate + a `db` module (e.g. `lambdas/src/db.rs` or a small `lambda-shared` module).
- Depends on `fbkl-entity` (re-exports `sea_orm`).

## What to do
1. Add `lambdas` to the workspace members.
2. Implement a `db()` accessor backed by `tokio::sync::OnceCell<DatabaseConnection>`:
   - Read `FBKL_DATABASE_URL` (must be Neon's `-pooler` endpoint at runtime).
   - `ConnectOptions`: `max_connections(1)`, `min_connections(0)`, `acquire_timeout(8s)`, `idle_timeout(300s)`, `max_lifetime(600s)`, `sqlx_logging(false)`.
   - Initialize once per execution environment; return `&'static DatabaseConnection`.
3. Add dependencies: `lambda_runtime`, `lambda_http` (version-matched to axum 0.8 — see gotcha), `tokio`, `serde_json`, `fbkl-entity`, `fbkl-server`, `fbkl-jobs`, `tracing`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `lambdas` crate builds in the workspace.
- [ ] `db()` compiles and returns a shared `DatabaseConnection`; calling it twice initializes the pool once.
- [ ] ConnectOptions match the values above.

## Verification
`cargo build -p lambdas`. Unit-test that two `db().await` calls return the same pool (pointer/identity or a connection-count probe).

## Notes / gotchas
- **Version compat is the real risk:** `lambda_http` must be built against `axum 0.8` / matching `http` crate, or the Router won't satisfy `Service<Request>`. Pin a compatible `lambda_http` release and document it.
- Pooled endpoint ONLY for Lambdas. Bypassing it (direct endpoint) → `FATAL: too many connections` with no queue.
close_reason: Added fbkl-lambdas crate to workspace with OnceCell-backed db() helper (max_conn=1,min=0,acquire=8s,idle=300s,max_lifetime=600s,sqlx_logging=false). lambda_http 0.14 + lambda_runtime 0.14.4 resolve to http 1.4.1, matching axum 0.8.9. Builds, clippy/fmt clean, identity test passes (skips w/o DB).

title: fbkl-api Lambda: Axum router via lambda_http + Function URL
description:
## Context
The HTTP API (GraphQL + login/registration/public routes) runs as a single Lambda wrapping the existing Axum router unchanged. Exposed via a Lambda Function URL (HTTPS), NOT API Gateway — Function URL is free indefinitely (API Gateway free tier is 12-month-only) and adds no stage-path prefix to rewrite.

## Files / locations
- New `lambdas/src/bin/api.rs`.
- Uses `fbkl_server::build_router`, `fbkl_server::AppState` (from T1) and `lambdas::db()` (from T2).

## What to do
1. `#[tokio::main] async fn main()`: init Lambda tracing subscriber (JSON, no ANSI), build `AppState { db: db().await.clone() }`, `let app = build_router(state).await;`, then `lambda_http::run(app).await`.
2. Configure the Lambda: arm64, `provided.al2023`, env vars `FBKL_DATABASE_URL` (pooled) + `SESSION_SECRET`, reserved concurrency ~50.
3. Enable Function URL; configure CORS (exact Pages origin, `Allow-Credentials: true`) — coordinate with the Pages/CORS task.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `cargo lambda build --release --arm64` produces the api bin.
- [ ] Deployed Function URL serves `/api/gql` (GraphiQL GET + POST) and the login flow.
- [ ] A login request sets the `fbkl_id` cookie; a subsequent authenticated GraphQL query succeeds using that cookie.
- [ ] Reserved concurrency is set on the function.

## Verification
`cargo lambda build --release --arm64`. After deploy: `curl` the Function URL `/api/gql` and run a login + authenticated query end-to-end (locally via `cargo lambda watch` first, then deployed).

## Notes / gotchas
- `lambda_http::run` accepts the Axum `Router` directly (it implements `Service<Request>`); the session + cookie + graphql layers ride along.
- Cookies need `SameSite=None; Secure` (already set) — Function URL is HTTPS so this works.
- Cross-origin cookie wiring is the most likely failure mode; see the Pages/CORS task.
notes: CODE DONE (pending Neon+AWS deploy to close): lambdas/src/bin/api.rs — tracing::init_default_subscriber, db() AppState, build_session_layer+build_graphql_schema+build_router, lambda_http::run(app). Compiles natively+clippy/fmt clean. Remaining: cargo-lambda arm64 build (toolchain not installed locally), Function URL + CORS + reserved-concurrency (deploy), cookie e2e verify.
close_reason: Serverless deploy epic complete: Lambdas live, EventBridge schedules firing, Neon DB, Pages + same-origin proxy auth, GitHub Actions CD.

title: Refactor server/ into reusable builders (lib-only) + keep local dev bin
description:
## Context
Foundation task. Today `server/src/main.rs` does everything inline: connect DB, build the Axum router, build the tower-sessions layer, build the async-graphql schema, `spawn_scheduler`, and `serve()` on :9001. To run the same app on Lambda we must extract these into reusable builder functions so both a local dev binary AND the Lambda binaries share one code path. No behavior change for local dev.

## Files / locations
- `server/src/main.rs` — source of the setup logic to extract (DB connect, session layer w/ SHA-512-derived key, graphql schema build, router assembly, `spawn_scheduler`).
- `server/src/lib.rs` — currently re-exports `graphql::*` and `server::*`; add the new builders here.
- `server/src/server.rs` — `AppState { db }` and `setup_server_router()` already exist; reuse.

## What to do
1. In `server/src/lib.rs`, add public async/sync builders:
   - `build_graphql_schema(db: DatabaseConnection) -> AppSchema` (move the `Schema::build(...).data(...).limit_complexity(50).limit_depth(10).finish()` block; expose a type alias for the schema).
   - `build_session_layer(db: &DatabaseConnection) -> SessionManagerLayer<PostgresStore>` (move the PostgresStore + SHA-512 key derivation + SameSite=None/Secure/Expiry config). Do NOT call `session_store.migrate()` here (moved to deploy/init, see notes).
   - `build_router(state: Arc<AppState>) -> Router` that composes `setup_server_router().with_state(state).layer(session_layer).layer(CookieManagerLayer::new()).layer(Extension(schema))`.
   - `init_db()` helper or accept a `DatabaseConnection` from the caller.
2. Remove `continuously_delete_expired` and `spawn_scheduler` from the shared builders — they become separate Lambdas. Keep them callable for the local bin.
3. Create `server/src/bin/local.rs` (or keep `main.rs` as the local bin) that reproduces today's behavior: connect DB, `build_router`, `spawn_scheduler`, run session-deletion loop, `serve()` on :9001 with graceful shutdown. Local DX must be unchanged (single process, 30s poll, no AWS).
4. Ensure `server` still builds as a lib that the new `lambdas` crate can depend on.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `cargo build` succeeds for the workspace.
- [ ] `cargo run --bin <local>` starts the server on :9001 with scheduler + session GC exactly as before.
- [ ] `build_router`, `build_graphql_schema`, `build_session_layer` are public and callable from another crate.
- [ ] No business logic moved or changed; only entrypoint wiring relocated.

## Verification
`cargo build && cargo clippy && cargo fmt --check`. Manually hit `http://localhost:9001/api/gql` (GraphiQL) and confirm login flow still sets the `fbkl_id` cookie.

## Notes / gotchas
- `session_store.migrate()` must still run somewhere — move it to a one-time deploy step / init, NOT per request and NOT per cold start. Track in the Neon/migrations task.
- Keep `setup()` (color_eyre + tracing init) usable by the local bin; Lambda bins init their own subscriber.
close_reason: Closed

title: Deploy FBKL to AWS Lambda + Neon + Cloudflare Pages (serverless, free-tier)
description:
## Goal
Stand up a production deployment of FBKL on permanently-free cloud tiers without managing custom infrastructure. Split the current single long-running Axum server into serverless components: a native-Rust HTTP API on AWS Lambda, scheduled/background work on Lambda triggered by EventBridge Scheduler, managed Postgres on Neon, and the two Vite frontends on Cloudflare Pages. Wire continuous deployment from `main` via GitHub Actions and add observability via CloudWatch + Sentry.

## Why this architecture
Evaluated Supabase, Cloudflare (Workers/Containers), Render, Koyeb, Fly, Railway. Conclusions:
- **Cloudflare Workers cannot run this stack** — they compile to `wasm32-unknown-unknown`, which has no `tokio` and fails to build `mio`/SeaORM/SQLx. Cloudflare Containers run native Rust but require Workers Paid ($5/mo) and Cron Triggers are paid-only.
- **Fly.io and Railway killed their free tiers** (2024/2023). Render free has cold starts and paid-only workers/cron.
- **AWS Lambda** runs native Rust (GA Nov 2025), 1M req + 400k GB-s/mo permanently free; **EventBridge Scheduler** gives 14M invocations/mo free — this closes the worker/cron gap nothing else did for free.
- **Neon** = always-free managed Postgres (10GB, no inactivity pause) with a built-in PgBouncer pooler endpoint — essential for Lambda connection management. (Supabase free pauses after 1 week idle; RDS free is 12-month-only + needs a paid NAT Gateway from Lambda.)
- **Cloudflare Pages** = best free static/SPA host (unlimited, global CDN, native SPA fallback). R2 not needed (no file uploads at this time).

## Migration is low-risk because the architecture already separates concerns
- `logic/`, `entity/`, `auth/`, `constants/`, `transaction-processor/`, `jobs/` are libraries reused UNCHANGED.
- `transaction-processor` is already generic over `C: ConnectionTrait + TransactionTrait` and idempotent via `job_run` claims — which makes EventBridge double-fires safe by construction.
- `jobs::run_scheduler_tick(&db)` is already directly callable (per its own doc comment) — it becomes the scheduler Lambda body verbatim.
- Only the binary entrypoints change; business logic does not.

## Confirmed decisions (from planning conversation)
- Scheduler cadence: accept EventBridge 1-minute minimum (down from the current 30s in-process poll).
- Frontends on Cloudflare Pages (not R2). No object storage at this time.
- Lambda reserved concurrency cap (~50 on the API) as the connection-pool backstop.
- One `lambdas` crate with three `[[bin]]` targets (api, scheduler, session-gc).
- Migrations run as a CD deploy step against Neon's DIRECT endpoint (pooler breaks DDL/advisory locks); app uses the POOLED endpoint.
- Observability: CloudWatch (infra) + Sentry free (app/frontend). Skip Datadog.
- CD via GitHub Actions on `main` push, path-filtered, with a manual approval gate on schema migrations.

## Key technical constraints (carry into every child)
- **DB pooling:** Lambdas MUST use Neon's `-pooler` endpoint (PgBouncer transaction mode). SeaORM `ConnectOptions`: `max_connections(1)`, `min_connections(0)`, `acquire_timeout ~8s`, `idle_timeout ~300s`, `max_lifetime ~600s`. Initialize once per execution env via `OnceCell` (reused across warm invocations). Cap Lambda reserved concurrency so worst-case client connections to the pooler stay bounded.
- **Sentry-on-Lambda:** Lambda freezes the execution env on handler return — MUST `sentry::flush()` with a timeout before returning or events are lost/delayed.
- **Cross-origin auth:** SPA (Pages) → API (Function URL) is cross-origin. Requires Function URL CORS with exact origin + `Allow-Credentials: true`, cookie `SameSite=None; Secure` (already set in `main.rs`), and `credentials: "include"` on the client. Prefer same parent domain (`app.` + `api.`) to keep cookies first-party.
- **Migrations:** backward-compatible / expand-contract only (old Lambda may serve mid-rollout). Never drop a column in the same deploy that stops using it.

## References
- Plan doc: this conversation (architecture evaluation + migration sketch), 2026-06-13.
- Source anchors: `server/src/main.rs` (setup to extract), `server/src/server.rs` (`setup_server_router`, `AppState`), `jobs/src/lib.rs` (`run_scheduler_tick`, `spawn_scheduler`), `transaction-processor/src/lib.rs` (`process_deadline`/`process_event`).

## Completion criteria
Epic is done when all child tasks are closed AND: pushing to `main` runs migrations (gated) → deploys all three Lambdas → deploys both Pages apps; the logged-in SPA authenticates against the API Lambda over HTTPS with working sessions; scheduled deadline processing fires via EventBridge; and errors surface in Sentry with CloudWatch alarms in place.
close_reason: Serverless deploy epic complete: Lambdas live, EventBridge schedules firing, Neon DB, Pages + same-origin proxy auth, GitHub Actions CD.

title: Ok(1) TBD-salary sentinel in annual_contract_advancement collides with real $1
description:
## Context
Surfaced during Phase 1 (fbkl-rust-0kb.1). `entity/src/entities/contract/annual_contract_advancement.rs` `calculate_yearly_salary_increase` returns `Ok(1)` as a "salary TBD, set later during veteran auction salary fetch" sentinel for UFA-original-team / UFA-veteran advancement (lines ~141, ~145). A real $1 salary is indistinguishable from TBD.

## Why deferred from Phase 1
The proper fix (return `Option<i16>`, `None` = TBD) requires the `contract.salary` DB column to be nullable — otherwise the sentinel just moves to the call site (`create_advancement_for_contract` sets `new_contract.salary = ActiveValue::Set(i16)`). Making the column nullable is a schema + round-trip change that belongs with Phase 4 illegal-states-unrepresentable type modeling, not the isolated Phase-1 crash fixes.

The Phase-1 crash on the same fn (`.to_string().parse().unwrap()` overflow at ~160) WAS fixed via `to_i16()` + propagation.

## What to do
- Make `contract.salary` nullable (migration) OR model salary as an enum that carries a TBD state.
- Return `Option<i16>` (or the enum) from `calculate_yearly_salary_increase`; `None`/TBD variant for the two UFA arms.
- Update the two test cases that currently assert `ActiveValue::Set(1)` (contract_advancement_r5_to_ufa20, contract_advancement_v3_to_ufa10).
- Update the veteran-auction salary-fetch path that later fills in the real salary.

## Acceptance
- No `Ok(1)` sentinel; TBD is a distinct type-level state from a real $1 salary.
- cargo build + test + clippy green.

title: Phase 4: illegal-states-unrepresentable type modeling
description:
Mutually-exclusive Option<i64> groups enforced only at runtime -> model as enums. Touches migrations/serialization; verify round-trip. See notes/idiomatic-rust-audit.md Phase 4. Biggest first:

- entity/src/entities/transaction/transaction_entity.rs:32-46 — 7 mutually-exclusive Option<i64>, each 1:1 with TransactionKind; fold ids into variants.
- entity/src/entities/trade_asset.rs:19-320 — 3 target ids gated by asset_type; enum TradeAssetTarget.
- entity/src/entities/contract/contract_entity.rs:54-63 — player-ref + lineage; enums.
- entity/src/entities/trade.rs:240-280 — trade lineage enum.
- entity/src/entities/team_update.rs:272-286 — transaction_id into Assets variant.
- entity/src/entities/team_user.rs:129-135 — final_end_of_season_year onto Inactive variant.
- entity/src/entities/contract/rookie_draft.rs:31-35 — is_league_player bool -> PlayerRef enum.
- entity/src/entities/contract/free_agent_extension.rs:37-61 — declare-then-assign + unreachable bail arm; single match expr + narrow param enum.
- server/src/graphql/contract/contract_types.rs:20-21 — push existing LeagueOrRealPlayer enum down into Contract.
- transaction-processor/src/lib.rs:42-124 — ProcessableEvent enum-as-struct -> struct + kind enum; :127-135 idempotency_key uses {:?} Debug for persisted key, use stable Display.

title: Phase 3: error architecture (LogicError, AuthError, entity consistency)
description:
See notes/idiomatic-rust-audit.md Phase 3.

- logic/ has no domain error enum; all color_eyre + ad-hoc strings. Add LogicError (thiserror) with per-mode variants, convert at boundaries. (Large.)
- auth/src/lib.rs:11,29,42,55 leak hex/argon2 dep error types; define AuthError with #[from], FbklError wraps that one type.
- Entity query error-type inconsistency: league_queries.rs, user_queries.rs, user_registration_queries.rs return raw Result<T,DbErr>; rest use color_eyre. Unify.
- server/src/session.rs:9 enforce_logged_in returns raw Result<i64,StatusCode>; return Result<i64,FbklError> (From<StatusCode> exists).
- Error-text convention (lowercase verb-led) batch: auction_bid.rs:100, team_update.rs:279, team_user.rs:135, trade.rs:246,261,274, contract_entity.rs:494-596, transaction_entity.rs:243-301, trade_asset.rs:265, transaction-processor/src/lib.rs:352-355.

title: Add Typography + Stack layout primitives (shadcn/@apply)
description: MUI migration dropped Typography/Stack/Box in favor of raw tags + repeated Tailwind strings. Re-introduce reusable Typography (variant prop -> @apply bundles, CVA, useRender polymorphism) and Stack layout (direction/gap/align/justify -> @apply, semantic gap scale) mirroring button.tsx pattern. Refactor all 8 migrated components/routes to use them.
close_reason: Added Typography + Stack primitives with @apply/CVA, refactored all 8 migrated components/routes. tsc + oxlint + vite build pass.

title: Port shadcn components to semantic classnames via @apply
description:
Emitted DOM from stock shadcn has utility-class bloat and classnames carry no meaning. Port webapp-logged-in's shadcn components (src/components/ui) so CVA maps variant props to semantic classnames (e.g. btn, btn--primary, btn--md) and the actual utility strings move into global.css under @layer components via @apply, composed from existing theme tokens.

Pattern:
- CVA stays as the typed prop API; variant values become semantic class names only.
- Styles transplanted verbatim from shadcn's CVA utility strings into @apply rules in global.css (or a dedicated components.css) — no redesign.
- Tailwind v4 layer order (components < utilities) preserves caller className overrides like px-2.

Scope: all installed components in webapp-logged-in/src/components/ui. Builds on feat/shadcn-migration branch (fbkl-rust-zhw).

Caveats to respect:
- tailwind-merge/cn cannot dedupe utilities against @apply'd classes; rely on layer order, add variants where same-layer conflicts appear.
- Future 'shadcn add' output arrives as utility strings and needs manual re-porting to this pattern.
- Raw utilities remain acceptable for one-off layout in feature code.
notes: Decision context: user dislikes tailwind classname bloat/loss of semantic meaning but likes shadcn default component styles. Chosen middle ground: semantic-CVA + @apply. Rejected: full CSS-in-JS/vanilla-extract rewrite (loses shadcn ecosystem), UnoCSS transformerCompileClass (hashed names, still non-semantic).
close_reason: Ported all 11 webapp-logged-in/src/components/ui components to semantic classnames (btn/badge/card/avatar/sheet/tooltip/sidebar-*) via CVA→class mapping + @layer components @apply in global.css. Markers (group/peer/@container), no-scrollbar, runtime-conditional utilities kept inline (@apply can't resolve them). Verified: vite build compiles all @apply; compiled CSS emits identical declarations per class; tsc/oxlint/oxfmt green. Commit fb45fd7. Force-closed: blocker zhw is code-complete on this branch (feat/shadcn-migration) with only its PR pending manual merge, so dv9's code dependency was satisfied.

title: Roster-lock cap: dropped-contract penalty over-rounds (196/195 false fail) + verify import halts
description: import-data Transactions EoSY2018 bails at deadline 109 team 10 (196/195). Suspect per-contract ceil rounding in salary_calculation.rs inflates dropped-contract penalty by 1 (15 vs 14). Also verify import hard-stops on bail (code says it does; user saw continuation -> likely stale binary).
notes: FINDINGS: (1) Cap 196/195 is NOT a logic bug. Rules doc 9.1.1-.2 (notes/2025-08-31-rules_document.md:230) mandates per-contract ceil(0.2*salary) drop penalty, NOT combined; spec-09 confirms current impl intended. So per-contract rounding in salary_calculation.rs:99 is correct. 196/195 = faithful data or stale DB. (2) Import HALTS on error: proven by code (pure ? propagation) AND live run (stopped at 2015 error, exit!=0, did not continue). User 'didn't stop' = stale binary/partial DB. (3) NEW BLOCKER: fresh replay (migration refresh + real-world + all-league-data) dies at EoSY2015 PreseasonStart advance_league_contracts with duplicate unique-active-contract violation (contract_queries.rs:412), well before deadline 109/2018. Collision created during advance_contract chain replacement. Means a clean replay cannot reach the reported 2018 error -> user's 196/195 came from a non-fresh DB. Reverted debug instrumentation in validate_rosters.rs.
close_reason: NOT-A-BUG on both reported symptoms. (1) 196/195 penalty: salary_calculation.rs:98 does per-contract ceil(0.2*salary), which rules 9.1.2 (notes/2025-08-31-rules_document.md:227) explicitly mandate ('Salaries may not be combined for drop penalties'). 196/195 is faithful to rules, not over-rounding. (2) Import halts on error: pure ? propagation (import-data) + prior live run confirmed halt with nonzero exit; user's 'didn't stop' was a stale binary/partial DB. Real remaining problem (EoSY2015 fresh-replay duplicate unique-active-contract crash) split out to fbkl-rust-bei; needs a live import run to root-cause. No code change.

title: DB integration test: scheduler per-league fail-closed barrier
description:
run_scheduler_tick (jobs/src/lib.rs) now processes deadlines per league, oldest-first, and stops a league's chain at the first deadline that doesn't reach Succeeded (Failed/AttemptsExhausted/AlreadyRunning/Err block later deadlines; AlreadyProcessed does not). No DB-backed test harness exists in jobs/, so this ordering/barrier is currently unverified by tests.

Need a DB integration test covering:
- Failed week-N deadline blocks week-(N+1) in same league (week N+1 left unattempted, counted in TickSummary.blocked).
- AttemptsExhausted and AlreadyRunning also block the chain.
- Successful chain processes all deadlines in datetime order.
- A stuck league does NOT block a different league (independence).
- AlreadyProcessed (race) does not block.

Likely needs a test Postgres (seed deadline + job_run rows) similar to other entity/logic DB tests if any, or a new harness.

title: Sentry observability: Rust Lambda + browser SPA
description:
Complete the app-level observability deferred from fbkl-rust-96e.9 (which shipped CloudWatch alarms + SNS only). Add Sentry error tracking to both the Rust Lambdas and the browser SPA.

## Why
CloudWatch covers infra metrics/alarms; Sentry gives app-level error context (stack traces, request context, release tracking) the epic called for but deferred pending a DSN.

## What to do
1. Create a Sentry project (free tier); obtain DSN(s).
2. Rust Lambdas: integrate sentry crate in lambdas/ bins. CRITICAL: Lambda freezes the execution env on handler return, so call sentry::flush() with a timeout BEFORE returning or events are lost (noted in epic constraints).
3. Browser SPA: add Sentry browser SDK to webapp-logged-in (+ optionally webapp-public).
4. Wire DSN via env: Lambda env var (Terraform, secret) + frontend build-time var.
5. Store DSN as GitHub secret if needed for frontend build in CD.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A thrown error in a Lambda appears in Sentry with a stack trace.
- [ ] A browser error in the SPA appears in Sentry.
- [ ] Lambda sentry::flush() runs before handler return (no dropped events).
- [ ] DSN provisioned via env/secret, not committed.

## Notes
- Neon Auth is separately deferred (see [[neon-auth-deferred]] memory) — unrelated.
- Reference: epic fbkl-rust-96e.9 deferred this.

title: Observability: CloudWatch alarms + Sentry (Rust Lambda + browser SPA)
description:
## Context
Two complementary planes. CloudWatch = infra (Lambda invocations, duration, errors, throttles, cold starts) with alarms — already present, ~free. Sentry free tier = app-level error grouping with stack traces + release health for both the Rust backend and the frontend SPAs. Skip Datadog (overkill, weak free tier, per-host billing punishes serverless).

## Files / locations
- Lambda bins (`lambdas/src/bin/*.rs`) — add Sentry init + flush.
- Both Vite apps — add Sentry browser SDK + source map upload at build.
- AWS CloudWatch alarms config.

## What to do
1. **CloudWatch alarms** on the API + scheduler Lambdas: error rate, throttles, p99 duration. Wire to an email/SNS notification.
2. **Sentry (Rust):** add the `sentry` crate to the Lambdas; init in each `main` with the DSN from env. CRITICAL: `sentry::flush(Some(timeout))` before each handler returns — Lambda freezes the env on return, so unflushed events are lost/delayed. Capture panics + `Err` paths (GraphQL resolver errors, processor failures).
3. **Sentry (frontend):** add the browser SDK to both apps; upload source maps during the build (needs `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN`) so traces de-minify. Tag releases with the git SHA.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CloudWatch alarms exist for API + scheduler error/throttle/duration and notify on breach.
- [ ] A deliberately-thrown backend error appears in Sentry WITH a usable stack trace (proves flush works on Lambda).
- [ ] A thrown frontend error appears in Sentry with de-minified frames.

## Verification
Trigger a forced error in a Lambda handler and a frontend route; confirm both land in Sentry with stacks. Trip a CloudWatch alarm (e.g. via forced throttle) and confirm the notification fires.

## Notes / gotchas
- Sentry-on-Lambda flush is the single most common reason "Sentry isn't catching Lambda errors." Do not skip the explicit flush.
- Sentry free tier (1 user, ~5k errors/mo, 30-day retention) is ample for this app.
notes: TF written: infra/observability.tf — Errors + Throttles CloudWatch alarms per Lambda (api/scheduler/session-gc) -> SNS topic fbkl-alerts -> email ohkaiby@gmail.com (must confirm subscription). Sentry SDK (Rust lambda flush-on-return + browser SPA) still TODO, needs DSN. Validated.
close_reason: Serverless deploy epic complete: Lambdas live, EventBridge schedules firing, Neon DB, Pages + same-origin proxy auth, GitHub Actions CD.

title: EventBridge Scheduler: 1-min scheduler + 5-min session-gc schedules
description:
## Context
Wire the two scheduled Lambdas to EventBridge Scheduler. Well within the 14M invocations/mo free tier.

## Files / locations
- AWS infra config (console, or IaC if adopted). Targets: the `fbkl-scheduler` and `fbkl-session-gc` Lambdas.

## What to do
1. Create schedule `fbkl-scheduler-tick` → `rate(1 minute)` → invoke `fbkl-scheduler`.
2. Create schedule `fbkl-session-gc` → `rate(5 minutes)` → invoke `fbkl-session-gc`.
3. Grant EventBridge Scheduler an IAM role allowed to invoke both functions.
4. Set flexible time window = off (or minimal) for the scheduler tick to keep cadence tight.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Both schedules exist and are enabled.
- [ ] CloudWatch shows the scheduler Lambda invoked ~once/minute and session-gc ~once/5-min.
- [ ] IAM role scoped to only invoke these two functions.

## Verification
Wait a few minutes; confirm invocation counts in CloudWatch metrics for both functions match the cadences.

## Notes / gotchas
- 1-minute is EventBridge's floor (accepted). If 30s ever becomes necessary, do two ticks per invocation instead — do not add a second schedule.
notes: TF written: infra/eventbridge.tf — aws_scheduler_schedule scheduler-tick rate(1m) + session-gc rate(5m), fbkl-scheduler-invoke role (scheduler.amazonaws.com, SourceAccount confused-deputy guard), retries=0. Validated. Pending apply (needs Neon URL + built zips).
close_reason: Serverless deploy epic complete: Lambdas live, EventBridge schedules firing, Neon DB, Pages + same-origin proxy auth, GitHub Actions CD.

title: fbkl-session-gc Lambda: delete_expired sessions on EventBridge (5-min)
description:
## Context
Replaces the in-process `session_store.continuously_delete_expired(60s)` loop with a Lambda that runs the one-shot `PostgresStore::delete_expired()` on a 5-minute EventBridge schedule.

## Files / locations
- New `lambdas/src/bin/session_gc.rs`.
- Reuses the session-store construction from T1 (extract a `build_session_store(db)` helper if needed) + `lambdas::db()`.

## What to do
1. `lambda_runtime::run(service_fn(handler))`; handler builds the `PostgresStore` over `db().await` and calls `.delete_expired().await?`.
2. Configure Lambda: arm64, `provided.al2023`, `FBKL_DATABASE_URL` (pooled), low concurrency.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `cargo lambda build` produces the session-gc bin.
- [ ] Invoking it deletes expired session rows (verify count drops in the `tower_sessions` table).

## Verification
Insert an expired session row in a test DB, invoke the function, confirm the row is gone.

## Notes / gotchas
- Ensure the tower-sessions schema exists (the `session_store.migrate()` step from T1/migrations task must have run).
close_reason: Serverless deploy epic complete: Lambdas live, EventBridge schedules firing, Neon DB, Pages + same-origin proxy auth, GitHub Actions CD.

Authed screens build-verified only (backend down). title: NBA schedule importer → season deadline generation
description: Deadline dates must be regenerated each NBA season from the upcoming schedule: preseason deadlines back-calculate from opening night, weekly roster locks must skip the All-Star break (full week with no regular-season games), and some days (e.g., MLK Day) have early-afternoon tip times. Today everything is hand-entered: HISTORICAL_SEASON_DATES_BY_END_OF_SEASON_YEAR in import-data/src/league/import_deadlines.rs (2015-2025) and NBA_ASB_DATE_RANGES_BY_END_OF_SEASON_YEAR in constants/src/date/all_star_break_dates.rs. Build an importer that fetches the official NBA schedule and derives these dates for the new end_of_season_year. NOT a blocker for P0 infra work: deadline scheduler/GraphQL operate on deadline table rows regardless of source, and the data source won't have the 2026-27 schedule until the NBA publishes it (~mid-August 2026).
design:
Data source (verified live 2026-06-09): GET https://cdn.nba.com/static/json/staticData/scheduleLeagueV2.json with browser User-Agent + 'Referer: https://www.nba.com/' headers (Akamai blocks bare requests). Returns leagueSchedule.gameDates[].games[] with per-game date/time (EST + UTC) for the full current season. stats.nba.com/stats/scheduleleaguev2 is unreliable; use the CDN endpoint.

Implementation:
- New module import-data/src/nba_schedule.rs: fetch via ureq with header spoofing (same pattern as existing neulion config fetch), parse into a SeasonSchedule struct of regular-season game dates + tip times. Filter games by gameId prefix: 001 = preseason, 002 = regular season.
- Derive: season start (earliest regular-season game), ASB date range (longest zero-game gap in Feb), last regular-season day / playoff start, early-start days (per-game tip times).
- Feed derived dates into deadline generation for the new end_of_season_year; keep hardcoded 2015-2025 historical data as-is.
- Reuse get_weekly_roster_locks() in import_deadlines.rs:939 — swap its hardcoded ASB lookup for the derived range.
- Cache raw JSON to import-data/src/data/ with dated filename (existing convention) so imports are reproducible offline.
- If the commissioner deadline-config UI (notes/2025-06-30-deadline_configuration.md) lands first, importer should produce PROPOSED deadlines for review rather than direct inserts.
acceptance_criteria: Running import with --data=deadlines for a new season derives season start, ASB range, playoff start, and early-start days from fetched scheduleLeagueV2.json instead of hand-entered constants; 2015-2025 historical imports unchanged; raw JSON cached to import-data/src/data/.

title: Phase 5: polish (newtype ids, DRY, iterators, clippy red)
description:

title: Replacement owners (ownership transfer + optional draft)
description: Implement per notes/implementation-specs/11-replacement-owners.md. Rules §2.4.

title: Self-host login hero image instead of hotlinking cdn.nba.com
description: login.tsx HERO_IMG hotlinks https://cdn.nba.com/manage/2025/12/GettyImages-2220888872-1024x1536.jpg as the login page background. nba.com can move/remove the URL or block hotlinking anytime, silently blanking the hero. Download a copy, add it to webapp-logged-in assets, and reference the local asset. Not a licensing issue (private app, friends-only) — purely reliability.
